days.title: Talking about tomorrow: ще + present

grammar.title: The future with ще + present tense
grammar.explanation: Bulgarian makes the future by placing the unchanging particle 'ще' before the normal present-tense verb. Unlike English, the verb still carries the personal ending: ще работя (I will work), ще работиш (you will work), ще работи (he/she will work). 'ще' itself never changes for person or number. There is no infinitive in Bulgarian, so you simply use the present form after ще. Note that Bulgarian has a real present-tense 'to be' (съм, си, е, сме, сте, са), and its future is ще съм / ще бъда — but for now focus on regular verbs.

reading.title: Планове за утре
reading.text: — Какво ще правиш утре? — Утре сутрин ще работя, а после ще почивам.
reading.translation: — What will you do tomorrow? — Tomorrow morning I will work, and afterwards I will rest.

days.speakingPrompt: Say three things you will do tomorrow using 'ще' + a present-tense verb. For example: Утре ще работя, ще чета и ще почивам.

days.title: Future for all persons: ще + present (plural)

vocab.exampleEn: In the evening we will rest.
Tomorrow we will study all day.

grammar.title: ще with we, you (plural) and they
grammar.explanation: The particle 'ще' stays the same for every person, but the verb takes the correct plural ending: ние ще пътуваме (we will travel), вие ще пътувате (you-plural will travel), те ще пътуват (they will travel). Word order is flexible, but 'ще' must come directly before the verb (or before a short pronoun + verb). Remember: Bulgarian has no noun cases, so the noun keeps the same form — relations come from prepositions like с (with), за (for), до (to/until).

examples.english: They will come in the evening.

reading.title: Една среща
reading.text: Утре ще имаме среща. Ние ще учим заедно целия ден, а вечер моят приятел ще дойде.
reading.translation: Tomorrow we will have a meeting. We will study together all day, and in the evening my friend will come.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe a plan you and a friend have for tomorrow using ние ще... Say at least two things you will do together.

days.title: Future time words: скоро and следващата седмица

grammar.title: Placing future time words in the sentence
grammar.explanation: Time words like скоро (soon), по-късно (later), тогава (then) and phrases like следващата седмица (next week) usually go at the start or end of the sentence; 'ще' stays glued to the verb. Notice the definite article as a suffix: седмица → седмицата, and on the adjective следваща → следващата (feminine -та). For masculine: месец → следващият месец; for feminine: година → следващата година. The article attaches to the first word of the noun phrase.

examples.english: Soon I will start a new plan.
Next week we will travel together.

reading.title: Скоро или по-късно
reading.text: — Кога ще пътуваш? — Не утре, а следващата седмица. Скоро ще започна нов план.
reading.translation: — When will you travel? — Not tomorrow, but next week. Soon I will start a new plan.

days.speakingPrompt: Talk about your plans using future time words. Say what you will do скоро, what you will do следващата седмица, and what you will do следващата година.

days.title: Saying you will NOT do something: няма да

vocab.exampleEn: Tomorrow I will stay at home.
I will not forget the meeting.

vocab.example: Няма да дойда, защото съм уморен.
vocab.exampleEn: I will not come because I am tired.

grammar.title: Negative future with няма да + present
grammar.explanation: To make the future negative you do NOT use 'не ще'. Instead you use the fixed phrase 'няма да' before the present-tense verb: positive ще работя → negative няма да работя (I will not work). 'няма' does not change for person; only the verb changes: няма да работя / работиш / работи / работим / работите / работят. The word 'да' here is the same particle used after искам, мога etc., and there is no infinitive in Bulgarian.

reading.title: Един спокоен ден
reading.text: Утре няма да работя. Ще остана вкъщи и ще почивам, защото съм уморен.
reading.translation: Tomorrow I will not work. I will stay at home and rest, because I am tired.

days.speakingPrompt: Say three things you will NOT do tomorrow using няма да. For example: Утре няма да работя, няма да пътувам и няма да уча.

days.title: Asking about the future: questions with ще and няма да

vocab.exampleEn: Tomorrow I will buy the ticket.

grammar.title: Yes/no and wh-questions in the future
grammar.explanation: For a yes/no question, put the particle 'ли' after the verb: Ще дойдеш ли? (Will you come?). For wh-questions, start with the question word: Кога ще дойдеш? (When will you come?), Защо няма да помогнеш? (Why won't you help?). With няма да the 'ли' goes after няма: Няма ли да дойдеш? (Won't you come?). Word order stays natural and no case endings change on the nouns; the definite article is still a suffix, e.g. билет → билета.

reading.title: Въпроси за пътуването
reading.text: — Кога ще пътуваш? — Следващата седмица. — Ще купиш ли билет днес? — Не, утре ще го купя.
reading.translation: — When will you travel? — Next week. — Will you buy a ticket today? — No, I will buy it tomorrow.

days.speakingPrompt: Ask a friend three questions about their future plans: one with ще...ли, one with кога, and one with защо няма да.

days.title: Putting it together: future plans and to be

vocab.exampleEn: Tomorrow I will be at home.
Next week I will be busy.
I am thinking about the future.

grammar.title: Future of 'to be' and mixing ще / няма да
grammar.explanation: Bulgarian has a present 'to be' (съм, си, е, сме, сте, са). Its future uses ще + the бъда forms: ще бъда, ще бъдеш, ще бъде, ще бъдем, ще бъдете, ще бъдат — or informally ще съм, ще си, ще е. The negative is няма да бъда / няма да съм. Now you can combine everything: positive (ще), negative (няма да), questions (ли), and time words. Remember adjectives agree with gender: свободен (m), свободна (f), свободно (n).

examples.russian: Следващата седмица няма да съм тук.
examples.english: Next week I will not be here.

reading.title: Мисли за бъдещето
reading.text: Утре ще бъда свободен, но следващата седмица ще бъда зает. Надявам се скоро да си починем заедно.
reading.translation: Tomorrow I will be free, but next week I will be busy. I hope we will soon rest together.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe your week ahead: say one day you ще бъдеш свободен, one day you ще бъдеш зает, and one thing you надяваш се да направиш (hope to do).

days.title: Review: The future

grammar.title: Recap: ще and няма да
grammar.explanation: This week you built the Bulgarian future. POSITIVE: ще + present-tense verb, with ще unchanging and the verb keeping its personal ending (ще работя, ще работим). NEGATIVE: няма да + present-tense verb (няма да работя, няма да работим) — never 'не ще'. QUESTIONS: yes/no with ли after the verb (Ще дойдеш ли?), wh-questions with кога, къде, защо. The verb 'to be' future is ще бъда / ще съм, negative няма да бъда / няма да съм. Time words: утре, скоро, по-късно, следващата седмица, следващия месец, следващата година. No noun cases anywhere; the definite article stays a suffix (билет → билета, седмица → седмицата).

examples.english: Soon we will start a new plan.

examples.russian: Следващата седмица няма да съм тук.
examples.english: Next week I will not be here.

reading.title: Планове за следващата седмица
reading.text: — Какво ще правиш следващата седмица? — Ще пътувам с приятел. — Ще бъдеш ли свободен в петък? — Не, в петък няма да съм тук, но скоро ще се видим.
reading.translation: — What will you do next week? — I will travel with a friend. — Will you be free on Friday? — No, on Friday I will not be here, but we will see each other soon.

days.speakingPrompt: Tell a short story about your next week: include one thing you ще направиш (positive future), one thing you няма да направиш (negative future), one question you would ask a friend, and at least two future time words (утре, скоро, следващата седмица).
